summary: Add OdooFieldServiceToolkit — a domain specialization over the existing OdooToolkit exposing 8 @tools for OCA fieldservice/fieldservice_stock route management. All required infrastructure (toolkit base, lazy transport, _execute RPC chokepoint, tool/permission decorators, Pydantic model layering, mature HITL/approval stack) already exists. The net-new work is FSM-specific Pydantic models (fsm.order/fsm.location entities + inputs + envelopes), 8 tool methods, and a decision on how 'rep confirm' and 'manager PIN' map onto the existing HITL/grant primitives.

localization.role: base class to extend; tool_prefix, lazy auth, __init__
RPC chokepoint for fsm/stock button methods + stage advance
reflection-based tool discovery — subclass methods auto-register
entity model pattern; add FsmOrder/FsmLocation here
central gating point for any approval/grant guard

constraints.claim: All tools must be async and go through OdooToolkit._execute / inherited helpers; no direct SDK or blocking I/O
Subclassing OdooToolkit auto-inherits ~30 generic CRUD tools; exposing them to a field rep may be undesirable (favor composition or tool filtering)
fsm.order / fsm.location entities, inputs, envelopes and known-model registration must be added — they do not exist today
Return pickings reuse existing stock.picking plumbing and attach_document for photos
No numeric-PIN primitive exists; manager PIN must map onto APPROVAL HITL, a FEAT-211 grant, or a new Odoo-credential check

hypothesis.statement: Implement OdooFieldServiceToolkit as a subclass of OdooToolkit adding 8 FSM @tools plus FSM Pydantic models, reusing _execute for RPC and the existing HumanInteractionManager (InteractionType.APPROVAL) for rep-confirm; gate the two PIN tools via FEAT-211 grants or APPROVAL, and expose only the FSM tool surface via get_tools_filtered.
hypothesis.reasoning: Every framework dependency exists and has repo precedent (DatabaseToolkit subclassing); only domain models + thin tool methods + one HITL wiring decision are net-new.

confidence_map.claim: OdooToolkit is the correct base; _execute is the RPC path
Subclass methods auto-register as tools via reflection
HITL APPROVAL covers rep-confirm out of the box
FSM models must be added (gap confirmed)
manager PIN maps to FEAT-211 grants/APPROVAL
rep_id resolves to fsm.order.person_id/user_id; Navigator sequence is a custom order field

unknowns.question: How should 'manager PIN' be implemented — reuse APPROVAL HITL (manager taps ✅), a FEAT-211 bounded grant, or a real numeric PIN verified against Odoo res.users?
unknowns.user_answer: Real numeric PIN verified against Odoo res.users (the `pin` arg is a true secret checked via Odoo credentials, e.g. check_credentials / a res.users PIN field). Needs a new credential-check path.

unknowns.question: Should OdooFieldServiceToolkit SUBCLASS OdooToolkit (inherits all ~30 generic CRUD tools) or COMPOSE/filter so only the 8 FSM tools are exposed to the rep?
unknowns.user_answer: Subclass OdooToolkit and expose all — the rep gets the 8 FSM tools plus all inherited generic CRUD tools. Least code, widest surface.

unknowns.question: Which fsm.order field encodes 'Navigator sequence' ordering, and how does rep_id map to fsm.order (person_id/user_id) and to the day's outbound pickings?
Which fsm.location field holds the 'planogram ref' returned by get_kiosk?

recommended_next_command.rationale: High-confidence localization with a clear, well-precedented implementation path. Remaining unknowns are bounded design/domain choices the spec can pin down in its Open Questions and Codebase Contract sections.
